message:
wide_string conformance: execute fixtures through harness subprocess matrix

Rewires crates/frankenlibc-harness/tests/wide_string_conformance_test.rs
so the fixture execution test drives the harness subprocess (matrix)
path instead of the in-process execute_fixture_case shortcut. This is
what bd-x5c9 tracks — the previous coverage left any subprocess-only
parity regressions invisible because the test never ran the same code
path the matrix runner uses.

Mechanics:
- Drop the frankenlibc_fixture_exec import; wide_string cases are now
  launched via env!("CARGO_BIN_EXE_harness") with the
  `conformance-matrix-case --function ... --mode ...` invocation,
  piping the JSON inputs through stdin and reading the JSON envelope
  on stdout. Stderr is surfaced on non-zero exit so parity divergence
  produces readable failure messages.
- Add local MatrixCaseEnvelope and DifferentialExecution deserialization
  structs so the test owns its on-wire contract; envelope kinds "ok"
  and "error" are both handled, with unknown kinds producing an
  explicit error.
- Rename wide_string_fixture_cases_match_execute_fixture_case to
  wide_string_fixture_executes_via_harness so the test's name reflects
  the subprocess path.
- Loosen the spec_section metadata check to accept either "ISO C" or
  "C11", matching the conventions already used in the fixture bodies.
- Tighten the parity assertion: the matrix path must produce
  host_parity=true (the in-process code had an "UB" escape hatch that
  hid real divergence behind an undefined-behavior sentinel).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
